input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T02:20:06.895Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (for exact type match) — the inline equivalent. Let me update your project:
data.reasoningText:
The user needs to replace `[ExpectedException]` with `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (MSTest v3.8+ / v4). In MSTest v4, the `[ExpectedException]` attribute was removed. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<TException>(() => ...)` or `Assert.ThrowsException<TException>(() => ...)`.

Let me update both the test file and the csproj to MSTest v4.

toolCalls.name: edit

toolResult.sessionLog:

index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-34d0eeefa1b2448b95647627827e40a6/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-34d0eeefa1b2448b95647627827e40a6/TestProject.csproj
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <ItemGroup>
-    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
+    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="4.3.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>
